title: 'They might be afraid of us!' Turkiye stars speak ahead of Socceroos clash, update on injured superstar as 24-year wait ends
original_text:
It might be Turkiye’s first appearance at a World Cup since 2002, but they’re embracing the tag of tournament dark horses as they relish their return to the world stage for the first time in 24 years.

The European outfit qualified through the UEFA playoffs to slot into Group D and present a high class examination of the Socceroos in the first encounter in Vancouver on Saturday (Sunday AEST).

Much of the focus on Group D has been on the second game against the USA thanks to the smack talk of the pundits in the States ruling the Socceroos out of contention.

But Turkiye, world No.22, present a formidable foe first.

Besiktas captain Orkun Kokcu says he has been dreaming of the day his nation returned to the World Cup stage and warns the rest of the tournament “might be afraid of us!”

“The atmosphere of friends is like a family atmosphere, we are very good. Everyone gets along well. This is reflected in the training. We are preparing in a very fun way. We are ready,” he said on Thursday.

“We will prepare even better. We are looking forward to the first match.

“You hear the stories of 2002; my father, my uncles tell them. Of course I couldn’t experience it, we participated in the tournament 24 years later. Because I was born and raised in the Netherlands, in 2010 the Netherlands reached the final, I saw the excitement there. I was dreaming that one day I would hopefully experience this with Turkey.

“Now we are on this stage. I hope we can experience it too.

“I agree that they see us as a ‘dark horse’, as a team we are full of quality players. We have players who can do something extra at any moment. They might be afraid of us!”

Fenerbahce winger Kerem Aktürkoğlu, who plays as a striker for his national team, talked about the responsibility of representing 85 million people and not just coming here to make up the numbers.

“We want to represent our country as a team and as a game. We are here after 24 years, we grew up with the stories of our older brothers being third in the world. It was the dream of all of us,” he said.

“It is a great pride to have achieved this, to have the opportunity to represent our country in the World Cup.

“Just coming here and participating should not be an achievement for a country like ours. After the European Championship, we are here with our football and as a team we reflect our own character and football on the field in every way. And this reflects positively on the results. He came to this tournament with a very good form graph.”

The major injury doubt surrounding the side is a big one: Juventus’ Kenan Yildiz.

Kökçü said, “Are you a Juventus fan? I think Kenan has a slight injury right now. We don’t know exactly. We all know how talented Kenan is. We need everyone. I hope we will see Kenan among us as soon as possible, Kenan is very important like the other players. I think he will be ready, I am not a doctor or physiotherapist, but he will be ready.”

Aktürkoğlu added: “We all know Kenan’s quality. He is very important for us.

“As far as I know, his injury is getting better, he started training. I don’t know the timing of his return as we are not doctors. I sincerely say that we have very good players, young and with potential. We are all aware of that. I hope Kenan will be with us in every game and make important contributions.

“It doesn’t really matter who plays, 85 million people will be behind him no matter who plays. I hope everyone will be fit and ready to play.”

David Weiner David Weiner is the Co-Founder of Golden Boot Media, publisher of football360.com.au. A domestic and international award-winning content leader, he was previously editor of Fox Sports Football and Optus Sport, as well as Director of Content at the A-Leagues. In 2024 he published the book The Goals That Changed Australia and now he is on a mission to turn football360.com.au into Australia’s go-to football media brand.
